scope: Diagnostic taxonomy only. It does not mark any journey or health report green.

source_inspection.observation: snapshot_registrations serializes commands, shortcuts, flags, providers, tool_defs, and models, but no event_handlers/event_hooks field. Event journeys therefore observe no event registrations even after a JS extension registers hooks.

EventSubscriber journey reads regs.event_handlers and fails with `Manifest expects event subscriptions but none registered` when the key is absent or empty while manifest capabilities.subscribes_events is non-empty.

The manifest extractor classifies every textual `.on(` as an event handler and copies those names into capabilities.subscribes_events. This catches pi.on hooks but also catches Node/EventEmitter listeners such as proc.stdout.on.

pi.on registers hooks in ext.hooks and the JS registration snapshot emits them as event_hooks. pi.events.on registers event-bus hooks separately, and the current snapshot shown here only exports ext.hooks.

ExtensionManager stores RegisterPayload.event_hooks, exposes list_event_hooks, and dispatches event payloads by matching extension event_hooks. Runtime storage exists; the generated journey snapshot is not reading it.

taxonomy.diagnosis: Generated journey tests expect regs.event_handlers but snapshot_registrations omits event hooks. Add event_handlers/event_hooks from ExtensionManager::list_event_hooks and keep naming aligned with VALIDATED_MANIFEST registrations.

The manifest extractor uses raw `.on(` text matching, so Node/EventEmitter listeners such as data/close/error can be misclassified as Pi event subscriptions. Tests should distinguish pi.on or pi.events.on from arbitrary object.on.

npm/pi-prompt-template-model sets registers_commands=true because source calls pi.registerCommand(name, ...), but command names are data-dependent on prompt files loaded from cwd. The hermetic journey cwd has no prompt inputs, so no commands are registered.

taxonomy.fix_type: runtime policy, package fixture, or compatibility decision
taxonomy.diagnosis: Health report failures include extensions attempting to create temp/state directories outside the extension root. Decide whether to provide an allowed state directory contract or keep denial and classify as unsupported.

taxonomy.fix_type: manifest/catalog reconciliation or runtime command bridge fix
taxonomy.diagnosis: Health report expects command `processes`, while runtime registers other commands. Reconcile artifact manifest expectations with loaded extension behavior.

trace_notes: Source lines tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/npm/pi-prompt-template-model/index.ts:451,456,495 register Pi event hooks.
Source line 539 calls pi.registerCommand(name, ...) inside a loop over loaded prompt files; hermetic conformance cwd produces no commands.

production_runtime_changes_required: Only if bd-d7v9g.2 finds ExtensionManager::list_event_hooks disagrees with JS registration payloads under a direct runtime regression. Current source inspection shows runtime hook storage/listing exists.
conformance_harness_changes_required: Normalize naming between VALIDATED_MANIFEST registrations.event_handlers and runtime RegisterPayload.event_hooks.
Tighten event subscription extraction so arbitrary object.on calls are not treated as Pi event subscriptions.
fixture_or_context_changes_required: Provide prompt files for npm/pi-prompt-template-model journey or classify dynamic prompt-derived commands separately from unconditional command registration.
report_contract_changes_required: Future journey details should include both expected and observed event handler arrays explicitly so absence of the snapshot key is visible.
